'''Zach Eckersley''' (born 10 November 2003) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} and {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]].

==Playing career==
===Wigan Warriors===
In August 2022, Eckersley made his [[Super League]] début for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62661463|title=Super League: Hull KR 38-28 Wigan Warriors - Home side hold on despite late flurry|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

====2023 loans====
:London Broncos
On 10 February 2023 it was announced he would join the [[London Broncos]] on an initial one month loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-harvie-hill-zack-eckersley-london-loan/|accessdate=10 Feb 2023|date=10 Feb 2023|title=Wigan duo head out on loan}}</ref>

:Widnes Vikings
On 4 April 2023 it was announced he would join the [[Widnes Vikings]] on  short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.widnesvikings.co.uk|url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/eckersley-joins-vikings-on-loan//|accessdate=4 Apr 2023|date=4 Apr 2023|title=Eckersley joins Vikings on loan}}</ref>

:Barrow Raiders
On 7 July 2023 it was announced he would join the [[Barrow Raiders]] on  short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2023/07/07/two-warriors-join-barrow-on-loan/|accessdate=1 Aug 2023|date=7 Jul 2023|title=Two Warriors join Barrow on loan|website=Wigan Warriors}}</ref>

====Return to Wigan====
On 8 June 2024, Eckersley started in the [[2024 Challenge Cup final]], scoring Wigan's opening try in the club's 18–8 victory over Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 30 May 2026, he played in Wigan's 2026 Challenge Cup final victory against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/01/challenge-cup-crowds-are-dwindling-but-rugby-league-must-save-wembley-relationship/|title=Challenge Cup crowds are dwindling but rugby league must save Wembley relationship|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
